pathophysiology_summary: Pemphigus foliaceus is a superficial autoimmune blistering disease (anti-desmoglein-1) producing crusted, scaly, erythematous patches in a seborrhoeic distribution (face, scalp, upper chest) with superficial peeling and a positive Nikolsky sign; intact blisters are rarely seen because they are so superficial. It is frequently misdiagnosed as seborrhoeic dermatitis, and harsh anti-dandruff shampoos/scrubbing aggravate it. Crucially — unlike pemphigus vulgaris and SJS — it spares the mucosa. The alarming spreading crusts raise fear of eczema herpeticum (punched-out erosions, systemic illness), severe impetigo (thick honey-coloured crusts, pus) and SJS (mucosal involvement, drug trigger), all of which are excluded here. Management is stopping the aggravating products, a topical corticosteroid and emollient, and dermatology confirmation (biopsy/immunofluorescence), with an infection/mucosal safety-net.

differential.evidence_basis: Superficial crusted scaly patches in a seborrhoeic distribution with peeling, aggravated by harsh shampoos.
Screen for punched-out erosions, grouped vesicles and systemic illness; absent.
Superficial peeling rather than thick honey-coloured purulent crusts.
Mucosal sparing and no drug trigger/systemic illness argue against SJS.
Mucosa clear, afebrile, no punched-out erosions (DI-001), superficial peeling crusts aggravated by harsh shampoos (DI-002) — pemphigus foliaceus.
No punched-out erosions, grouped vesicles or systemic illness (safety-net retained).
Mucosa spared and no drug trigger/systemic illness.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: stop harsh shampoos/cleansers, topical corticosteroid + bland emollient, dermatology confirmation (biopsy/immunofluorescence), and an eczema-herpeticum/mucosal/infection safety-net. ED over-triage of a superficial afebrile eruption is unnecessary.

red_flags.description: Eczema herpeticum/SJS/secondary infection — punched-out erosions or grouped vesicles, mucosal (mouth/eye) involvement, fever, or spreading purulence
red_flags.clinical_significance: Herpeticum/SJS/severe infection are serious and need urgent antiviral/antibiotic or emergency care.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Assuming a spreading crusted eruption is a 'flesh-eating' infection and over-triaging, without noting the superficial, mucosa-sparing, afebrile picture.
Continuing harsh anti-dandruff shampoos, which aggravate pemphigus foliaceus.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Nikolsky testing and skin biopsy with direct immunofluorescence
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Stop harsh products; topical steroid + emollient; dermatology confirmation; eczema-herpeticum/mucosal/infection safety-net.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Over-triaging a benign superficial eruption wastes resources; missing a genuine eczema herpeticum/secondary infection would be dangerous (hence the retained safety-net).
